technique: Separate quant + afp4wfp4_preshuffle for small-M split-K shapes
reason: Extra kernel launch + memory traffic for writing/reading A_fp4 (57KB) and A_scale (1KB) costs ~5us, completely negating any XCD remap benefit from the afp4wfp4_preshuffle kernel

technique: BSN=64 for M<=32 K<=1024 fused preshuffle
reason: BSN=64 NW=4 with BSM=8: +1.0% geomean regression. Per-block compute efficiency loss from smaller N-tiles (64 vs 128) outweighs CU utilization gain from doubled grid (180 vs 90 blocks). M=32 shapes with K=512 are at a fixed cost floor.

waves_per_eu=2 for M<=4/8 (v205: +0.9%), waves_per_eu=0 for M=32 (v203: +1.4%), waves_per_eu=1 for split-K (v204: +1.2%) all regressed. Only M=64 1->2 was beneficial.
